context: I can tell you that a bear with a brush in it mouth could paint a fence almost as well as Lautisse.
Then he come in, with paint on he clothes and on he hand and face.
"Come in," he say, "I be afraid you will be greatly surprise when you see what we have do.
But before I can begin, Lautisse come into the garden.
Lautisse come on the twelve o'clock train and I meet he at the station.
He come to I and show I a silver pen.
He plan to spend a month in New York, where nobody know he, and Betsy invite he to come to our place in the country for a week-end.

"No, no!" he cry, and he go on, work more quick.
"Do not you know that some of the rich person in the country be collector of Lautisse painting? This be only a fence, but it be a real Lautisse!" I hold up I hand.
" He make I come with he to look at he work.
"I have enjoy read about your fence so much," he say after he have introduce himself, "that I simply have to come and look at it.
